Overview

...

CRM

We recommend PipelineSprout CRM and SalesNest Lite.

Project Management

We feature SprintCloud and RemoteBoard.

Marketing & automation

StarterMail Automate is our pick for lightweight automation.

Finance & Expense Tracking

For tracking spend, ExpenseFox and InvoiceRocket are our top choices. Read full review and Read full review.

Customer Support

HelpHive Desk and ChatLoop Support cover shared inbox and live chat needs.

tools.name: PipelineSprout CRM
tools.description: A lightweight, founder-friendly CRM designed for 3–10 person startup sales teams with deep Gmail integration.
tools.summary: Simple pipeline CRM with Gmail sync and automation for small startup teams.
pros: Very fast to set up for small teams
Excellent Gmail integration and email logging
Clear, simple pipeline views

tools.name: SalesNest Lite
tools.description: An affordable CRM with built-in email templates and basic automation, ideal for early-stage SaaS sales teams.
tools.summary: Affordable CRM with email templates and pipeline automation.
pros: Strong value at the entry-level price
Good balance of features without being overwhelming
cons: Automation is basic compared to enterprise CRMs

tools.name: FoundersGrid CRM
tools.description: A CRM built for founder-led sales motions with strong integrations and customizable pipelines.
tools.summary: Customizable CRM for founder-led sales motions.
pros: Highly configurable pipelines
Solid integration ecosystem
cons: Setup can take longer than simpler CRMs

tools.name: DealFlowHQ
tools.description: Pipeline-centric CRM with visual boards and simple automation rules.
tools.summary: Visual pipeline CRM with simple automation.
pros: Very easy to use Kanban-style pipeline
No long-term contracts

tools.name: SprintCloud Tasks
tools.description: Project management built for remote engineering and product teams, with built-in time tracking and workload views.
tools.summary: Remote-first project management with built-in time tracking.
tools.free_plan_description: Free for up to 3 users with basic boards and time tracking.
pros: Excellent time tracking and reporting for remote teams
Tight integration with developer tools
Generous free tier for very small teams
cons: Interface may feel complex to non-technical teams

tools.name: RemoteBoard Projects
tools.description: A collaboration and project hub for remote teams with built-in time tracking and async updates.
tools.summary: Collaborative project hub with time tracking for remote teams.
pros: Strong support for async communication
Built-in time tracking with flexible reports
cons: Limited native integrations beyond core tools

tools.name: KanbanJet
tools.description: A simple Kanban board tool with checklists and due dates for small teams.
tools.summary: Straightforward Kanban boards for startups that want minimal overhead.
tools.free_plan_description: Free for up to 2 boards and 3 collaborators.
pros: Very easy to onboard non-technical users
Affordable pricing for tiny teams

tools.name: InvoiceRocket
tools.description: Invoicing software with unlimited invoices, multi-currency support, and simple expense tracking.
tools.summary: Unlimited, multi-currency invoicing under $15/month.
pros: Unlimited invoices even on the lowest tier
Supports clients in multiple currencies
Clean, easy-to-use interface

tools.name: BillWave
tools.description: Simple invoicing and payment tracking for local businesses charging in a single currency.
tools.summary: Budget invoicing for local, single-currency startups.
tools.free_plan_description: Free for up to 10 invoices per month.
pros: Very low price point
Good for local service businesses
cons: Invoice volume is limited on lower tiers

tools.name: GlobalBill Pro
tools.description: Advanced invoicing platform with unlimited invoices, multi-currency support, and tax handling for international clients.
tools.summary: Unlimited, tax-friendly invoicing for global client bases.
pros: Unlimited invoices and clients on all paid plans
Robust tax and compliance features for cross-border billing
cons: More complex than lightweight invoicing apps

tools.name: ExpenseFox
tools.description: Expense tracking and receipt capture for early-stage startups and remote teams.
tools.summary: Simple expense tracking with multi-currency support.
tools.free_plan_description: Free for up to 100 expenses per month.
pros: Very low per-user price
Multi-currency expense tracking for distributed teams
cons: Basic reporting compared to full accounting suites

tools.name: LedgerLeaf
tools.description: Full double-entry accounting tailored to SaaS startups with subscription revenue.
tools.summary: SaaS-focused accounting with revenue recognition and metrics.
pros: Purpose-built for SaaS accounting and metrics
Good integrations with subscription analytics tools
cons: Steeper learning curve than basic bookkeeping tools
More expensive than simple invoicing apps

tools.name: HelpHive Desk
tools.description: Shared inbox, ticketing, and live chat in one tool with a generous free-forever plan.
tools.summary: Shared inbox and live chat with a generous free plan.
tools.free_plan_description: Free for up to 3 support agents with shared inbox and live chat.
pros: Robust free tier for early-stage teams
Fast, modern chat widget
Good Slack notifications for new tickets
cons: Reporting is limited on free and lower tiers

tools.name: ChatLoop Support
tools.description: Live chat-centric support platform with bots, canned responses, and a free-forever tier.
tools.summary: Live chat-first support platform with bots and a free tier.
tools.free_plan_description: Free plan includes 1 chat widget and up to 500 chats/month.
pros: Strong live chat and bot capabilities
Free plan is enough for many early-stage startups
cons: Email ticketing is basic compared to dedicated helpdesks

tools.name: SupportNest
tools.description: All-in-one support tool with shared inbox, live chat, and a lightweight knowledge base.
tools.summary: All-in-one support with live chat and knowledge base.
tools.free_plan_description: Free for 2 agents with basic shared inbox and live chat.
pros: Combines shared inbox, chat, and docs in one tool
Easy to onboard non-technical team members
cons: Limited customization of the help center
Free plan lacks advanced automation rules

tools.name: TicketStream
tools.description: Ticketing-first helpdesk with SLAs, advanced routing, and multi-brand support.
tools.summary: Ticketing-first helpdesk for teams that have outgrown shared inboxes.
pros: Powerful routing and SLA features
Good fit for larger support teams
cons: No free tier, only a trial
Implementation can be heavy for small startups

tools.name: LaunchFlow Automate
tools.description: Marketing automation for SaaS startups with visual workflows, event-based triggers, and strong product usage integrations.
tools.summary: Visual workflow automation for product-led SaaS startups.
tools.free_plan_description: Free up to 1,000 contacts with core automation.
pros: Great balance of power and simplicity for early-stage SaaS
Deep product analytics integrations
cons: Templates library is smaller than incumbents
Can get pricey as contact count grows

tools.name: GrowthLoop Drip
tools.description: Email and SMS automation platform focused on lifecycle campaigns and behavioral targeting.
tools.summary: High-deliverability email and SMS automation for startups.
pros: Excellent deliverability for email campaigns
Strong SMS capabilities alongside email
Robust segmentation options

tools.name: StarterMail Automate
tools.description: Entry-level email automation built specifically for very small teams sending newsletters and simple drips.
tools.summary: Entry-level email automation for very small teams.
pros: Extremely simple for non-marketers
Very affordable for small lists

tools.name: ScaleMark 360
tools.description: Enterprise-grade marketing automation packed into a startup-friendly interface, with multi-channel campaigns and advanced analytics.
tools.summary: Multi-channel marketing automation with advanced analytics.
pros: Very powerful automation and analytics capabilities
Good fit for later-stage startups and scale-ups
cons: Pricing starts higher than other tools in this list
Initial setup and onboarding can be complex
